notes:
SPECIAL MINI-SET (NOT a standard BW booster). Dragon Vault (dv1), English release 2012-10-05, BW-era special expansion, 21 cards (20 Normal #1/20-20/20 + 1 Secret #21/20 Kyurem). The chase card is Kyurem 21/20 (secret).

PACK STRUCTURE (well-sourced, the load-bearing fact): Bulbapedia Dragon Vault page (fetched) states each booster pack contained EXACTLY 5 HOLOFOIL cards and that ALL cards in the set are holographic ("intentionally designed with every card as a holofoil, creating a premium product"). There is NO common/uncommon/reverse-holo/rare-slot structure here - it is not a normal 10-card BW pack. So the standard BW baseline (1 rare + 3 unc + 5 com + 1 RH) does NOT apply and was deliberately NOT used. packSize=5, every slot draws a holofoil from the same all-holo 21-card pool.

DISTRIBUTION MODEL: With every card foil and packs being 5 random holos from a 21-card all-foil pool, each of the 5 slots is modeled as a uniform draw across the set proportional to bucket size. Catalog buckets (given) = {rare-holo:18, rare:2, common:1} (=21 cards; these labels are the engine classifier's split of the otherwise unmarked all-holo set - the cards carry "no rarity markings" per pokellector/Bulbapedia, all are holographic). Proportional draw: rare-holo = 18/21 = 0.857, rare = 2/21 = 0.095, common = 1/21 = 0.048. Sums to 0.857+0.095+0.048 = 1.000 exactly. All 5 slots use this identical distribution (5 independent foil pulls from the same pool). This is a STRUCTURAL consequence of "all cards holo, 5 per pack" - not an invented per-card percentage.

DISTRIBUTION (NO published per-set rates were invented): no per-rarity-tier pull study exists because the set has only one effective rarity tier (all holo). The keys {rare-holo, rare, common} are a strict subset of the available buckets {rare-holo, rare, common}. Every slot sums to exactly 1.0.

PRODUCTS: Bulbapedia confirms Dragon Vault was NOT sold in traditional booster boxes - "only made available in the Dragon Vault Blisters released on October 5, 2012." There was NO 36-pack booster box and NO ETB. The sold unit was a single-pack blister (1 pack of 5 holos + a Holographic promo). display.packs = 1 (one blister pack); etb = null. Sealed Dragon Vault blisters/setbox carry a collector premium with the Kyurem chase, so EV/pack will sit below sealed prices - expected, rates not inflated.

CONFIDENCE: 'medium'. The pack structure (5 holofoil cards/pack, all-holo set, blister-only) is directly and clearly sourced from Bulbapedia, and the uniform-draw distribution is a direct structural consequence rather than an estimated hit-rate; but there is no large published statistical sample of opened Dragon Vault packs, so not 'high'.
